title: Roasted Juicy Croatian Lentils Dal
description: A hearty and flavorful one-pan dish featuring roasted lentils, vegetables, and a tangy tomato base, perfect for a cold winter evening. This Croatian-inspired meal is plant-based-friendly and can be prepared in under an hour.

ingredients.name: Red Bell Pepper
ingredients.notes: seeded and roughly chopped, to roast together with the lentils.

ingredients.name: Carrots
ingredients.notes: peeled and roughly chopped, to roast together with the lentils.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: minced, to add flavor during the last few minutes of roasting.

ingredients.name: Turmeric
ingredients.notes: ground, to add warmth and depth to the dish.

ingredients.name: Paprika
ingredients.notes: sweet or smoked, to add a smoky flavor.

ingredients.name: Tomato Paste
ingredients.notes: unsweetened, to deglaze the pan and add a tangy base.

ingredients.name: Red Wine Vinegar
ingredients.notes: to deglaze the pan and add a tangy flavor.

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: to achieve the desired consistency and prevent sticking.

ingredients.name: Fresh Cilantro

ingredients.notes: to season and bring out the flavors.

ingredients.notes: to roast the lentils and vegetables, and prevent sticking.

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large kadhai over medium heat. Add the onion, bell pepper, and carrots, and roast for 10-12 minutes or until the vegetables are tender and lightly browned, turning occasionally.
Stir in the garlic, turmeric, paprika, and salt, and cook for 1-2 minutes or until fragrant.
Add the green red lentils to the kadhai and stir well to coat with the spice mixture.
Roast for a further 5-7 minutes or until the lentils are tender and lightly browned, stirring occasionally.
Add the tomato paste and red wine vinegar to the kadhai, and stir to deglaze the pan, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Add the water to the kadhai and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook for 20-25 minutes or until the lentils are tender and the liquid has been absorbed, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the coconut milk, chopped cilantro, and salt, and cook for a further 2-3 minutes or until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed, adding more salt, black pepper, or herbs to taste.
Serve the roasted lentils dal hot, garnished with chopped cilantro and a dollop of yogurt or sour cream, if desired.
